_meta.chapter: Zoning Ordinance (adopted Apr 21, 2010; updated through Aug 14, 2023)
_meta.contact: Lukas Hill, Community Development Director — lhill@springlaketwp.org — (616) 842-1340
_meta.notes: Density figures are derived from minimum lot area per dwelling unit — no explicit units/acre limits appear in the ordinance. All multi-family and two-family dwellings require connection to public water and public sewer (Section 355). Developments of 8+ lots or site condo units in RR/R-1/R-2/R-3/R-4 MUST proceed as PUD (Section 332). R-1/R-2 densities shown for with-sewer condition (development assumption).

AG.label: Agricultural District
AG.density_basis: 2-acre minimum lot — 0.5 u/ac theoretical but no residential development pathway to ≥3 u/ac. Treated as 0 for screening.
AG.notes: Single-family only; agricultural district. No path to ≥3 u/ac.

RR.label: Rural Residential District
RR.density_basis: 2-acre minimum lot — 0.5 u/ac theoretical. No PUD listed as SLU in RR; large-scale developments require PUD per Section 332 but PUD density cannot exceed base zoning. No path to ≥3 u/ac.
RR.notes: Single-family only. Natural feature protection emphasis (dunes, riparian, woodlands). PUD not listed as SLU; however Section 332 requires 8+ lot developments to proceed as PUD — verify with Township. PUD density bonus would not get this zone to ≥3 u/ac.

R-1.label: Low Density Residential-Resource District
R-1.density_basis: 15,000 sq ft min lot with public sewer = ~2.9 u/ac (floor → 2). Without sewer: 43,560 sqft = 1 u/ac. With-sewer condition assumed for development screening.
R-1.notes: Single-family only by right. PUD is listed as special land use (Section 938) — allows unit type flexibility and density bonus (+25%) on ≥5 acre sites. 8+ lot developments MUST go PUD (Section 332). Critical Dune setbacks may apply; varied setback standards depending on waterfront/dune location.

R-2.label: Medium Density Residential-Suburban District
R-2.density_basis: 12,000 sq ft min lot with public sewer = ~3.6 u/ac (floor → 3). Without sewer: 43,560 sqft = 1 u/ac. Sidewalks, bike paths, recreation open space emphasis.
R-2.notes: Single-family only by right. PUD (Section 938) available for flexibility and density bonus (+25%). 8+ lot developments MUST go PUD (Section 332). Walkable suburban pattern intended.

R-3.label: Medium Density Residential-Cottage District
R-3.density_basis: 12,000 sq ft min lot = ~3.6 u/ac (floor → 3). Served with public water and sewer. Cottage/summer-vacationer settlement area (Strawberry Point).
R-3.notes: Single-family only. Front setback may be reduced to average of existing dwellings within 200 ft (min 15 ft). Higher lot coverage (40%) reflects cottage character. PUD available.

R-4.label: High Density Residential District
R-4.density_basis: 3,500 sq ft min lot area per DU (multifamily) = ~12.4 u/ac. No explicit cap stated. Public water and sewer required for MF.
R-4.notes: Two-family and multi-family by right. Senior housing by right. Assisted living as special use. PUD density bonus (+25%) available. Multi-family design standards (Section 331) apply — balconies, ADA access, facade articulation, exterior materials.

NC.label: Neighborhood Commercial District
NC.density_basis: No explicit residential density standard in NC; multi-family permitted by right. Using R-4 proxy (3,500 sq ft/DU = ~12.4 u/ac). Verify with Township whether R-4 multi-family standards apply.
NC.notes: Multifamily by right (confirmed in Table 402.A and Section 411). Density standard for MF not explicitly stated in NC section — contact Township to confirm. Light commercial intent; small scale; local consumers.

GC.label: General Commercial District
GC.density_basis: No residential uses permitted per Table 402.A.
GC.notes: No residential uses. Minor arterial/collector commercial.

MU.label: Mixed Use Commercial District
MU.density_basis: 3,500 sq ft min lot area per DU (multifamily) = ~12.4 u/ac. First floor must be non-residential. Public water and sewer required.
MU.notes: Mixed-use nodes at major intersections. Multifamily by right above non-residential. First floor must be non-residential for non-MF buildings. Build-to-zone: 70% of frontage. Ground floor transparency 60–85%. Separate residential entrance required. Multi-family design standards (Section 331) apply.

I.label: Industrial District
I.notes: No residential uses. Raw materials processing.

PR.label: Public/Recreation District
PR.density_basis: No residential uses permitted. Parks, schools, cemeteries, golf course.

MH.label: Mobile Home District
MH.density_basis: State-regulated per Michigan Mobile Home Commission Act (Act 96 of 1987). No local density limit. Treated as 0 for conventional development screening.
MH.notes: Mobile home parks only. State-regulated; not a conventional residential development pathway.

pd_process.section: N/A — Spring Lake Township does not have a PD zone district
pd_process.notes: Spring Lake Township uses PUD (Planned Unit Development) as a special land use rather than a separate zone district. See prd_special_use section.

prd_special_use.label: Planned Unit Development (PUD)
prd_special_use.approval_required: Planning Commission (public hearing + recommendation) + Township Board (final approval)

NC.basis: Using R-4 proxy; density bonus may apply

density_bonus_conditions: Site contains unique natural features or large open spaces
PUD exceeds minimum SCA (Sustainable Community Assessment) point standard
prd_special_use.large_scale_rule: 8+ lot/unit developments in RR/R-1/R-2/R-3/R-4 MUST proceed as PUD (Section 332)
prd_special_use.notes: PUD base density cannot exceed underlying zone density. Density Bonus Option (Section 1411) allows up to +25% units when open space exceeds 20% minimum. R-1 parcels ≥5 acres can reach ≥3 u/ac via PUD with density bonus. Construction must begin within 1 year of final plan approval.

affordable_housing.notes: No mandatory inclusionary zoning. Affordable housing is an optional scoring factor in the SCA points system (Article 13) for discretionary approvals — earns points but is not required.
